title: Preflight run-gate + setup/check modes
summary: Make `golemic run` execute preflight (read-only check mode) before any GitHub access, failing closed with exit 1 and English `FAILED:` output to stderr. Split preflight into setup (writes/scaffolds, gh-api login comparison) and check (read-only, local token-value comparison) modes. Runner obtains preflight via an injectable interface so unit tests inject a passing stub. Prepares issue e2e-test-preflight-failures (003).

stakeholder_intent.goal: Have `golemic run` reject a broken environment early via a read-only preflight, before touching GitHub
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Prevents silent failures and unintended GitHub actions when prerequisites are missing; unblocks the 003 E2E preflight-failure test which asserts run fails closed
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: On broken env: exit 1, `FAILED: <check>` lines + `failed` on stderr, zero run artifacts, no GitHub access. On valid env: preflight passes (`ok`) and the normal run proceeds unchanged.

in_scope: Add a read-only `check` mode to preflight (no file writes, no scaffolding)
Token distinctness in check mode via local value comparison (dev_token == reviewer_token -> fail)
Gate `golemic run` on preflight check mode after host-repo resolution, before event-log/GitHub access
Expose `golemic preflight --check` (read-only); `golemic preflight` without flag keeps current setup/scaffold behavior
Introduce an injectable Preflighter interface consumed by the runner; existing runner unit tests inject a passing stub
Standardize preflight output to English: per-check `OK: <name>` / `FAILED: <name> - <detail>`, final summary line `ok` / `failed`; in run, failure output goes to stderr
Update existing preflight/main/runner tests to the new output and gate
out_of_scope: The E2E preflight-failure test itself (issue e2e-test-preflight-failures / 003)
Changing the set of checks or their order
Keeping the gh-api login comparison in the run path (it stays in setup mode only)
New standalone check-only subcommand beyond the --check flag

preconditions: internal/preflight already runs 6 checks in fixed order (gh, pi, git, scaffolding, config, credentials)
`golemic preflight` command exists and scaffolds .golemic/ + credentials skeleton

business_rules.rule: `golemic run` must run preflight in check mode and fail closed on the first failing check, before any event-log creation or GitHub access
business_rules.outcome: On failure: exit 1, no run artifacts, no GitHub access

business_rules.rule: Check mode is strictly read-only: it never scaffolds or writes files. A missing .golemic/config.json or credentials file is a FAILED result, not a trigger to create it
In check mode, token distinctness is verified by local value comparison (dev_token == reviewer_token -> FAILED). The gh-api login comparison remains only in setup mode
business_rules.applies_when: Credentials check runs in check mode
business_rules.outcome: Identical token values rejected locally with no GitHub call

business_rules.rule: Preflight output is English: one line per check `OK: <name>` or `FAILED: <name> - <detail>`, followed by a final summary line `ok` (all passed) or `failed` (any failed). In the run gate, failure output goes to stderr; standalone `golemic preflight[ --check]` writes to stdout
The runner obtains preflight through an injectable interface (Preflighter). Production wires the real check-mode implementation; tests inject a stub
business_rules.outcome: Runner unit tests need not mock gh/pi/git preflight commands

inputs.name: environment
constraints: PATH, env vars, .golemic/ may be broken

outputs.body: stderr: FAILED: <name> - <detail> lines + `failed`; no run artifacts

inputs.name: --check
constraints: when set: read-only, no scaffolding, local token comparison

outputs.body: scaffolds .golemic/ + credentials skeleton as today; gh-api login comparison; OK/FAILED lines + ok/failed to stdout
read-only checks, no writes, local token-value comparison; OK/FAILED lines + ok/failed to stdout
Results with per-check Ok/Details; Results.AllOK() drives the gate

errors.message: failures are represented as non-Ok Results, not Go errors

process_steps.name: Resolve host repo
process_steps.state_before: cwd known, repoRoot not yet resolved

process_steps.name: Run preflight check (read-only)
process_steps.action: Invoke injected Preflighter.Check() which runs the 6 checks in order in read-only mode (no scaffolding, local token-value comparison)
process_steps.failure_behavior: proceed to PS-003 with non-OK results

process_steps.name: Fail closed
process_steps.action: Write FAILED: <name> - <detail> lines + `failed` to stderr; return exit 1 without creating run dir, event log, or run_started, and without any GitHub access

process_steps.name: Proceed with run
process_steps.action: Continue the existing run sequence unchanged (config load, credentials, event log, GitHub ops)

security.authentication: Credentials resolved from env/credentials file; token values must never appear in output
authorization_rules: No special authorization; token values must not leak into stdout/stderr

integration_points: Runner.Run() gate insertion after resolveHostRepo (internal/runner/runner.go:282), before event-log creation (:311)
preflight.RunAll() -> add mode parameter/variant for check vs setup (internal/preflight/preflight.go:100)
architecture_constraints: External commands (gh, pi, git) stay behind the injectable Executor interface (§2.12)
Never overwrite human-edited files; check mode writes nothing
Do not change the set or order of the 6 checks
allowed_changes: internal/preflight: add check/setup mode, English output, local token-value comparison
internal/runner: add injectable Preflighter, gate Run()
Update affected unit tests (preflight, main, runner) to new output and gate
forbidden_changes: Making the run path perform the gh-api login comparison
dependencies: Depends on completed happy-path orchestration (precondition, already deleted from backlog)
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Additive + output-format change; existing preflight tests updated in the same slice

acceptance_scenarios.title: run fails closed when a prerequisite is missing
given: A Preflighter that reports a failing check (e.g. gh missing)
then: stderr contains 'FAILED: ' for the failing check and the final line 'failed'
No run directory / event log / run_started is created

acceptance_scenarios.title: identical token values rejected locally in check mode
given: dev_token and reviewer_token resolve to the same value
when: preflight runs in check mode (via run or --check)
then: Credentials check is FAILED with a 'tokens identical' detail
No gh api user call is made for the distinctness decision

acceptance_scenarios.title: run proceeds unchanged when preflight passes
given: A Preflighter that reports all checks OK
then: The gate passes and the existing run sequence runs unchanged
run_started event and run directory are created as before

acceptance_scenarios.title: check mode writes nothing
then: Exit code 1 with a FAILED line for the missing scaffolding/config
No .golemic/ directory, config.json, or credentials file is created

acceptance_scenarios.title: setup mode (no flag) still scaffolds
then: Scaffolding is created as today (regression: existing behavior preserved)
Result is FAILED with 'wurde angelegt' style prompt to fill templates

acceptance_scenarios.title: output format and stream
given: A mix of passing and failing checks
then: Final summary line is 'ok' when all pass, otherwise 'failed'
In the run gate, failure output is on stderr; standalone preflight writes to stdout

test_cases: check mode: each of the 6 checks failing yields non-OK Results and writes no files
check mode: identical token values -> FAILED without gh api user call
runner gate: failing Preflighter stub -> exit 1, no run dir/event log/GitHub access
runner gate: passing Preflighter stub -> normal run proceeds
output: OK:/FAILED: per-check lines and final ok/failed summary; run failures on stderr
preflight --check flag parsed; setup (no flag) still scaffolds
existing preflight/main/runner tests updated to new output and gate stay green
non_functional_requirements: Check mode is local and fast (<1s); no network calls for the token-distinctness decision
definition_of_done: golemic run runs preflight check before event-log/GitHub access and fails closed (exit 1, stderr, zero artifacts)
Preflight has read-only check mode and writing setup mode; golemic preflight --check is read-only
Token distinctness in check mode uses local value comparison
Runner consumes an injectable Preflighter; runner unit tests inject a stub
Preflight output is English OK:/FAILED: + ok/failed; run failures on stderr
All affected existing tests updated; go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... green

decision_log.decision: golemic run always runs preflight (check mode) before any work; overrides the earlier 'no production changes' assumption
decision_log.rationale: Maintainer wants fail-closed behavior at the run boundary; 003's premise (run runs the checks) is only true after this change

decision_log.decision: Check mode compares token values locally (no gh api user); login comparison stays in setup mode
decision_log.rationale: Keeps run fast and network-free before abort; satisfies 003 AC 'both tokens same value'

decision_log.decision: golemic preflight = setup (writes); golemic preflight --check = read-only; run uses check internally
decision_log.rationale: Explicit flag exposes the read-only path without a new subcommand

decision_log.decision: Runner gets an injectable Preflighter interface; existing runner tests inject a passing stub
decision_log.rationale: Avoids rewriting ~20 runner fakeExecutor tests to mock gh/pi/git preflight commands

decision_log.decision: On preflight failure in run: immediate abort, zero artifacts (no run dir/event log/run_started)
decision_log.rationale: Clean fail-closed; matches 'aborts before GitHub access'

decision_log.decision: English output: per-check OK:/FAILED: <name> - <detail>, final summary ok/failed; run failures to stderr
decision_log.rationale: Deterministic English strings for the 003 asserts; replaces German OK:/FEHLT:/SUCCESS

codebase_evidence.finding: Run() does resolveHostRepo (:282) -> config.Load (:291) -> credentials.Load (:300) -> event log (:311) -> run_started (:326) -> GitHub. No preflight checks today; gate must go after :282 and before :311
RunAll runs the 6 checks in order and prints 'OK: <name>' / 'FEHLT: <name> — <details>' (German) to p.stdout
checkCredentials scaffolds credentials.json if missing, calls gh api user for both tokens and compares GitHub logins (:404). This GitHub-hitting comparison is what must stay in setup mode only
runPreflight builds Preflight, SetStdout(stdout), RunAll(), prints 'SUCCESS' + exit 0 when AllOK else exit 1. This is where the --check flag and setup/check selection wire in

Runner tests use a fakeExecutor that only mocks git subcommands and gh issue/pr. Adding a concrete preflight to Run() would make all of them fail with 'not mocked' for gh/pi --version etc — hence the injectable Preflighter stub
Existing tests assert exact German strings 'OK: gh installiert', 'SUCCESS', 'FEHLT: gh installiert — ...'. These must be updated to the new English OK:/FAILED: + ok/failed format
